amplify.branch.AppId.Description: The unique ID for an Amplify app.

amplify.branch.BasicAuthConfig.Description: The basic authorization credentials for a branch of an Amplify app. You must base64-encode the authorization credentials and provide them in the format `user:password`.

amplify.branch.BuildSpec.Description: The build specification (build spec) for the branch.

amplify.branch.Description.Description: The description for the branch that is part of an Amplify app.

amplify.branch.EnableAutoBuild.Description: Enables auto building for the branch.

amplify.branch.EnablePerformanceMode.Description: Enables performance mode for the branch.  Performance mode optimizes for faster hosting performance by keeping content cached at the edge for a longer interval. When performance mode is enabled, hosting configuration or code changes can take up to 10 minutes to roll out.

amplify.branch.EnablePullRequestPreview.Description: Sets whether the Amplify Console creates a preview for each pull request that is made for this branch. If this property is enabled, the Amplify Console deploys your app to a unique preview URL after each pull request is opened. Development and QA teams can use this preview to test the pull request before it's merged into a production or integration branch.  To provide backend support for your preview, the Amplify Console automatically provisions a temporary backend environment that it deletes when the pull request is closed. If you want to specify a dedicated backend environment for your previews, use the `PullRequestEnvironmentName` property.  For more information, see [Web Previews](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amplify/latest/userguide/pr-previews.html) in the *AWS Amplify Console User Guide*.

amplify.branch.EnvironmentVariables.Description: The environment variables for the branch.

amplify.branch.PullRequestEnvironmentNam.Description: If pull request previews are enabled for this branch, you can use this property to specify a dedicated backend environment for your previews. For example, you could specify an environment named `prod`, `test`, or `dev` that you initialized with the Amplify CLI and mapped to this branch.  To enable pull request previews, set the `EnablePullRequestPreview` property to `true`.  If you don't specify an environment, the Amplify Console provides backend support for each preview by automatically provisioning a temporary backend environment. Amplify Console deletes this environment when the pull request is closed.  For more information about creating backend environments, see [Feature Branch Deployments and Team Workflows](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amplify/latest/userguide/multi-environments.html) in the *AWS Amplify Console User Guide*.

amplify.branch.Stage.Description: Describes the current stage for the branch.
